questions.question_text: You are required to turn right at the signal-controlled junction. There is no traffic directly behind you. How should you proceed?

answers.1: Remain in your lane up to the pedestrian crossing.
answers.2: Move into the bus lane and remain there up to the pedestrian crossing.
answers.3: Cross the bus lane and position the vehicle close to the kerb before the cycle path.

questions.question_text: You are driving at the maximum permitted speed. What requires particular attention here?

answers.4: That 80 km/h remains permitted after the sign.

questions.question_text: You intend to turn right here. There are no cyclists on the cycle path behind you. What should you do now?

answers.3: Wait for the oncoming cyclist to pass.
answers.4: Be aware that you are about to cross a two-lane cycle path.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h, matching the speed of the driver ahead. How should you continue?

answers.1: Continue at approximately 50 km/h without adjustment.
answers.2: Increase your distance from the vehicle ahead.
answers.3: Be particularly aware of the surface condition of the traffic lane.

questions.question_text: What requires particular attention here?
You intend to turn left at this intersection. How should you proceed?

answers.1: Pay particular attention to oncoming traffic.
answers.2: Proceed to the centre of the intersection.

questions.question_text: Which lights must be used here?

answers.3: Use dipped headlights or main beam.
answers.4: Automatic daytime running lights are sufficient.

questions.question_text: You are stationary and intend to move off from the roadside. What should you do?

answers.2: Pay particular attention to oncoming traffic.
answers.3: Turn your head and check whether the road is clear behind you.
